questions.question: Which of the following numbers is an integer but is neither positive nor negative?
questions.explanation: Zero is a whole number and therefore an integer, but it is neutral—neither positive nor negative.

questions.question: Choose the correct comparison symbol: $-15$ ____ $-12$.
questions.explanation: On a horizontal number line, $-15$ lies further to the left than $-12$, which makes $-15$ less than $-12$ ($-15 < -12$).

questions.question: If a checking account is overdrawn by $\$50$ (meaning the account is in debt), which integer represents this balance?
questions.explanation: An overdrawn state represents debt, which is represented by a negative integer: $-50$.

questions.question: On a standard horizontal number line, negative numbers always lie:
questions.explanation: Negative numbers are values less than zero and lie to the left of zero on a horizontal number line.

questions.question: Arrange these integers in ascending order: $-3, \ -8, \ 2, \ -1$.
questions.explanation: Arranging from smallest (most negative) to largest gives: $-8, -3, -1, 2$.
